title: Gaslit to the Limit
theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.
omen: a florist delivery nobody ordered arriving already open

starter_premise.title: Gaslit to the Limit
starter_premise.premise: The therapist is sleeping with the husband or on his payroll and every session leaves her more confused.
starter_premise.signature_tell: Therapy scenes always end with her leaving more confused than she arrived.
starter_premise.base_premise: She knows something is wrong, everyone calls her crazy, and she is not crazy.

props: throat lozenges with a suspicious aftertaste

cast.name: Jenna Bennett
traits: works too hard to notice the affair until everyone else does
keeps a color-coded calendar and a cracked heart

cast.space_description: dock office with a desk nobody fully claims
cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

beat_sheet.text: The asymmetry is visible to the audience before it is legible to Jenna Bennett: The therapist is sleeping with the husband or on his payroll and every session leaves her more confused. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Elias Pike hesitates before explaining old storm shelter from before the divorce. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

The first wrong read happens in public, and from then on the county starts accumulating evidence in the wrong emotional direction. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.09. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

Signature tell: Therapy scenes always end with her leaving more confused than she arrived. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. The county now treats the missing information as proof against her instead of proof of manipulation. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.99. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

By the end of act two, survival depends on moving while still partially ignorant. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Jenna Bennett hesitates before explaining dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.17.

The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

A chase or confrontation near St. Agnes Regional forces action before comprehension is complete. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.81. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

Justice is partial or pyrrhic. The visible conspiracy breaks, but the informational gap stays open somewhere at the edge of the county. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 3.07. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Elias Lark is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Jenna Bennett says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Alder Ridge.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Elias?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Rachel. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Jenna. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The throat lozenges with a suspicious aftertaste is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Luke."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "Gaslit to the Limit understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "Gaslit to the Limit" follows Jenna Bennett, a high-output betrayed wife caught inside wrong roommate dead-sister resemblance spiral before she didn't know yet. Elias Lark collides with her life carrying portable suspicion, plausible access to offsite storage, and the kind of calm that sounds premeditated in a dim kitchen. The governing injury is simple: The therapist is sleeping with the husband or on his payroll and every session leaves her more confused. The signature tell is always this: Therapy scenes always end with her leaving more confused than she arrived. As a florist delivery nobody ordered arriving already open, what she cannot yet see becomes the syntax of the whole county, and every withheld fact keeps breathing just ahead of her. Where every secret comes home.
